Elevated cholesterol levels with intermittent chest tightness.
Patient is undergoing treatment and advised to attend regular follow-up visits.
Blood pressure readings are consistently elevated.

prescriptions.chiefComplaint: Intermittent chest pain, shortness of breath during exertion, and occasional palpitations for the past two weeks.

medicines.name: Nitroglycerin 0.5 mg
medicines.instructions: Place under tongue during chest pain

prescriptions.dietAdvice: Follow a low-sodium, low-fat diet. Increase intake of fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and maintain adequate hydration.
prescriptions.exerciseAdvice: Perform brisk walking for 30 minutes daily, five days per week, as tolerated by the patient.
prescriptions.lifestyleAdvice: Avoid smoking and alcohol consumption, manage stress through relaxation techniques, maintain healthy sleep habits, and attend regular cardiology follow-up visits.

prescriptions.chiefComplaint: Patient is undergoing treatment and advised to attend regular follow-up visits.

medicines.name: Cisplatin
medicines.instructions: Hydration required before and after dose

prescriptions.dietAdvice: High-protein diet, fresh fruits, green vegetables, plenty of fluids, avoid processed and junk food
prescriptions.exerciseAdvice: Light walking (15–20 minutes daily), breathing exercises, avoid heavy physical strain
prescriptions.lifestyleAdvice: Take proper rest, avoid stress, maintain hygiene, attend regular chemotherapy sessions, follow doctor’s schedule strictly

prescriptions.chiefComplaint: Patient experience frequent headaches, dizziness, and elevated blood pressure readings over the last month. Reports increased stress at work

medicines.name: Amlodipine
medicines.instructions: Take at the same time daily. Do not skip dose.

medicines.name: Atorvastatin
medicines.instructions: Avoid oily food and report muscle pain if any.

medicines.name: Aspirin
medicines.instructions: Take only after food. Avoid if gastric bleeding symptoms occur.

prescriptions.dietAdvice: Follow a low-salt, low-fat diet. Avoid fried foods, processed foods, excess tea or coffee, and high-sodium packaged snacks. Include fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and adequate water intake.
prescriptions.exerciseAdvice: Do 30 minutes of light walking daily for at least 5 days a week. Avoid heavy exercise until blood pressure is controlled.
prescriptions.lifestyleAdvice: Reduce stress, sleep 7-8 hours daily, avoid smoking and alcohol, monitor blood pressure at home twice daily, and maintain a healthy body weight.
prescriptions.doctorAdvice: Continue medicines regularly. Check blood pressure every morning and evening. Visit emergency care immediately if severe chest pain, breathing difficulty, fainting, or very high blood pressure occurs.
prescriptions.followUpNotes: Review blood pressure chart, ECG report, lipid profile, and kidney function test in the next follow-up. Adjust medicine dose if BP remains above 140/90 mmHg.

messages.text: Hello Ganesh, how is your health?

medicalReports.symptoms: Chest pain, shortness of breath, fatigue, dizziness, irregular heartbeat.
medicalReports.description: Patient underwent cardiac assessment due to recurring chest discomfort and shortness of breath. ECG and cardiac examination findings suggest mild coronary artery disease requiring continuous monitoring.
medicalReports.doctorNotes: Patient is advised to follow a low-fat diet, maintain regular physical activity, and avoid smoking. Follow-up consultation is recommended after four weeks along with repeat cardiac investigations.

medicalReports.symptoms: Persistent fatigue, unexplained weight loss, loss of appetite, and occasional pain.
medicalReports.description: Diagnostic findings indicate malignant tissue changes requiring further oncological evaluation and treatment planning.
medicalReports.doctorNotes: Patient is stable and advised to continue prescribed therapy with regular follow-up visits.

medicalReports.symptoms: Headache, dizziness, chest discomfort, fatigue, blurred vision, palpitations, shortness of breath
medicalReports.diagnosis: Hypertension with mild left ventricular strain
medicalReports.description: Patient presented with elevated blood pressure readings and symptoms of headache, dizziness, occasional chest discomfort, and fatigue. Clinical evaluation suggest hypertension requiring regular monitoring and lifestyle modification
medicalReports.doctorNotes: Advise patient to monitor blood pressure twice daily, follow a low-salt diet, avoid smoking and alcohol, reduce stress, and continue prescribed antihypertensive medicines. Follow up consultation is recommended after two weeks with ECG, lipid profile, and kidney function test reports.

medicalReports.symptoms: Headache, dizziness, chest discomfort, fatigue, blurred vision, palpitations, shortness of breath
medicalReports.diagnosis: Hypertension with mild left ventricular strain

medicalReports.doctorNotes: Advance patient to monitor blood pressure twice daily, follow a low-salt diet, avoid smoking and alcohol, reduce stress, and continue prescribed antihypertensive medicines.

recommendations: Track blood pressure and sugar daily.

aiSuggestions.summary: Based on your health profile, here are personalized AI health suggestions.

suggestions.title: Maintain a balanced diet
suggestions.description: Eat more fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and drink enough water daily.

suggestions.title: Do light physical activity
suggestions.description: Walk for 20–30 minutes daily unless your doctor advised rest.

suggestions.title: Follow up with doctor
suggestions.description: Continue regular health checkups and follow prescribed medicines.

riskAlerts.title: Do not ignore severe symptoms
riskAlerts.message: If you have chest pain, breathing difficulty, fainting, or severe pain, contact a doctor immediately.
